mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and equity risk premia, so declines in policy rates should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL without a matching rise in FEDFUNDS tightens real policy conditions, compresses valuation multiples, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged for at least 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign disappears once FEDFUNDS is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE reflects weakening payroll income and recession risk, which should lead equity investors to mark down earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if positive 5-day SPX returns occur more often than negative returns after unemployment increases, or if the event study effect is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE falls, labor income and consumption momentum improve together, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not higher than unconditional averages.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS signals improving nominal final demand and stronger revenue growth, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS growth is followed by negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses predictive power in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in INDPRO after industrial weakness signals cyclical production reacceleration and better profit leverage, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after at least 2 months of stagnation
failure_condition: Reject if production upturns fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the return premium vanishes once GDP and retail data are considered.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after prior weakness marks an early-cycle improvement in demand-sensitive activity, and that inflection should lead positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month over month after being below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing upturn events do not show higher forward SPX returns than non-events, or if the sign reverses during the same regime.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and RSAFS both soften together, the economy is entering a broad demand slowdown that should hurt earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS weakness does not coincide with below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone fully explains the effect.
